talk_title: AI‑Guided Discovery and Targeting of Plexin B2 in Circulating Tumor Cell Clusters

targets.dependency_context: CTC clusters in triple‑negative breast cancer

biomarkers.name: PLEXIN B2 expression
biomarkers.finding: High plexin B2 in CTC clusters correlates with reduced distant metastasis‑free survival (HR 1.8, p=0.02)

models.name: BALB/c spontaneous metastasis model
models.genotype_or_key_features: 4T1 breast cancer cells, plexin B2 knockout vs control

MOA_summary: AI‑driven ranking (R‑score) identified plexin B2 as a top adhesion molecule driving homotypic and heterotypic CTC cluster formation; neutralizing antibodies block plexin B2, reducing cluster size and number, decreasing lung metastasis in mice, and correlating with improved patient outcomes.
key_findings: R‑score prioritized plexin B2 among 30 adhesion proteins as the top CTC‑cluster driver.
Genetic knockout of plexin B2 in 4T1 cells reduces lung micro‑metastases by ~70% (p<0.001).
Anti‑plexin B2 antibodies decrease both homotypic tumor‑tumor and heterotypic tumor‑myeloid clusters in vitro.
Single‑dose anti‑plexin B2 treatment in BALB/c mice cuts metastatic burden by 60% (p<0.01).
High plexin B2 expression in patient CTC clusters associates with worse distant metastasis‑free survival.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Antibody cross‑reacts with mouse plexin B2, enabling preclinical testing; no off‑target toxicity observed in normal tissues.

combination_strategies.rationale: Potential additive effect by reducing CTC‑mediated dissemination
combination_strategies.phenotype: Preliminary in vivo data suggest enhanced tumor control (not yet quantified)

open_questions: Can anti‑plexin B2 therapy overcome resistance to existing ADCs in patients with low nectin‑4?
What is the durability of the anti‑cluster effect after repeated dosing?

talk_title: SWALL‑1 Controls Epigenetic Methylation, Mitochondrial Metabolism, and Antitumor Immunity in IDH‑Mutant Gliomas

biomarkers.name: SWELL1 expression
biomarkers.finding: IDH‑mutant gliomas show ~1‑fold higher SWELL1 mRNA vs wild‑type (TCGA).

biomarkers.name: D‑2HG extracellular level
biomarkers.finding: SWELL1 knockout reduces extracellular D‑2HG by ~45% in vitro.

MOA_summary: SWELL1 forms the LRRC8A‑C VRAC complex that mediates passive export of the oncometabolite D‑2HG from IDH‑mutant glioma cells; loss of SWELL1 reduces extracellular D‑2HG, enhances cGAS‑STING activation, increases CD8⁺ T‑cell infiltration, and suppresses tumor proliferation via hyper‑methylation of MYC/SOX2 pathways and impaired mitochondrial respiration.
key_findings: SWELL1 knockdown decreases extracellular D‑2HG release by ~40% without affecting LDH release.
LRRC8C knockdown reduces D‑2HG release ~25%; LRRC8D knockdown increases release, indicating subunit specificity.
SWELL1‑KO IDH‑mut glioma xenografts show prolonged mouse survival (median 45 vs 30 days, p=0.004).
SWELL1 loss leads to increased microglial CD86⁺ activation and CD8⁺ T‑cell IFN‑γ⁺ frequency.
SWELL1‑KO cells accumulate intracellular D‑2HG (~2‑fold) and display increased H3K27me3 and DNA hyper‑methylation, down‑regulating MYC and SOX2.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: SWELL1 is ubiquitously expressed; tumor specificity derives from reliance on D‑2HG export in IDH‑mut context.

combination_strategies.rationale: SWELL1 loss enhances antitumor immunity via cGAS‑STING
combination_strategies.phenotype: Synergistic tumor control in orthotopic models (not yet quantified)

open_questions: Will pharmacologic inhibition of VRAC recapitulate genetic SWELL1 loss?
Can SWELL1 expression serve as a biomarker for response to IDH inhibitors?

talk_title: Lineage Plasticity Drives Antibody‑Drug Conjugate Resistance in Bladder Cancer

biomarkers.name: NECTIN‑4 expression
biomarkers.finding: High NECTIN‑4 tumors have median EV treatment duration 9.2 months vs 3.1 months for low‑expressors (p=0.001).

biomarkers.name: ASCL1 activity
biomarkers.finding: ASCL1 overexpression suppresses NECTIN‑4 transcription in HT1376 cells (−70% mRNA, p<0.01).

models.name: 300‑patient bladder tumor cohort (MSK)
models.genotype_or_key_features: Urothelial vs variant histology, NECTIN‑4 IHC

MOA_summary: Lineage plasticity driven by ASCL1/NEUROD1 reprogramming suppresses NECTIN‑4 expression, rendering enfortumab vedotin ineffective; variant histology tumors up‑regulate alternative surface antigens such as DL3, offering new ADC targets for combination therapy.
key_findings: ASCL1/NEUROD1 overexpression in HT1376 cells reduces NECTIN‑4 mRNA by 70% and abolishes EV‑mediated cytotoxicity.
Spatial transcriptomics shows NECTIN‑4 loss confined to neuroendocrine regions, while DL3 is up‑regulated there.
Patients with NECTIN‑4‑low variant histology have significantly shorter EV treatment duration.
Proposed combination of NECTIN‑4 ADC with DL3‑targeted ADC could address heterogeneous antigen expression.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Plasticity‑mediated antigen loss is reversible in vitro upon ASCL1 knockdown, suggesting epigenetic regulation.

combination_strategies.rationale: Compensate for NECTIN‑4 loss in neuroendocrine/sarcomatoid subclones
combination_strategies.phenotype: Predicted synergistic tumor control in mixed‑histology models

open_questions: Can epigenetic drugs restore NECTIN‑4 expression in resistant tumors?
What is the optimal sequencing of dual‑ADC therapy to prevent clonal escape?

talk_title: eIF4A1 Inhibition Suppresses AML via Serine Biosynthesis Blockade

targets.dependency_context: AML cells with high eIF4A1 expression
AML cells sensitized by eIF4A1 inhibition

biomarkers.name: PHGDH expression
biomarkers.finding: eIF4A1 knockout reduces PHGDH mRNA by 65% (p<0.001).

biomarkers.name: Serine levels
biomarkers.finding: Intracellular serine drops 45% after zoledronate treatment.

MOA_summary: Zoledronate inhibits eIF4A1, destabilizing YB‑1‑bound mRNAs such as PHGDH, leading to reduced serine biosynthesis, impaired oxidative phosphorylation, and AML cell death; combined with MCL1 inhibition yields synergistic cytotoxicity.
key_findings: eIF4A1 is the most overexpressed translation initiation factor in AML vs normal hematopoietic cells.
eIF4A1 knockout prolongs survival in immunocompetent AML mouse model (median 60 vs 38 days, p=0.003).
Zoledronate kills AML cell lines with nanomolar IC50 while sparing normal CD34⁺ cells.
PHGDH down‑regulation mediates serine depletion; overexpression rescues growth inhibition.
CRISPR screen identifies YB‑1 and MCL1 as top sensitizers; zoledronate + MCL1 inhibitor shows >2‑fold synergy (CI<0.5).
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Zoledronate shows minimal toxicity to normal hematopoietic cells; selectivity linked to AML‑specific eIF4A1 dependence.

combination_strategies.rationale: MCL1 knockdown sensitizes AML to eIF4A1 inhibition
YB‑1 is a co‑factor for eIF4A1‑mediated mRNA stability
combination_strategies.phenotype: Enhanced AML cell death in vitro

open_questions: Will zoledronate retain selectivity in a broader panel of normal tissues in vivo?
Can eIF4A1 inhibition overcome resistance to standard AML therapies (e.g., venetoclax)?

talk_title: Targeting Rho1/2 to Overcome ADC Resistance in Triple‑Negative Breast Cancer

biomarkers.name: RHO2 expression
biomarkers.finding: 70% of HR‑negative breast cancers express surface RHO2 by CITE‑seq.

models.name: RHO2+ sorted cells
models.genotype_or_key_features: 5,000 cells injected generate tumors; RHO2‑ cells do not

MOA_summary: Anti‑RHO1/2 antibodies bind surface oncogenic embryonic proteins re‑expressed in TNBC stem‑like cells, suppressing stemness signatures, NF‑κB signaling, and NRF2‑driven antioxidant response, thereby sensitizing cells to the redox‑modulating agent APR‑246.
key_findings: RHO2+ sorted TNBC cells generate tumors at 5,000 cells; RHO2‑ cells fail to engraft.
Anti‑RHO2 treatment reduces NF‑κB p‑p65 and NQO1 levels in vitro.
Combination of anti‑RHO2 antibody with APR‑246 lowers APR‑246 IC50 by 2.5‑fold in TNBC cell lines.
In vivo, anti‑RHO2 alone modestly slows tumor growth; combined with APR‑246 yields significant tumor regression (p<0.01).
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: RHO1/2 are embryonic proteins absent in adult normal tissues, suggesting a favorable therapeutic window.

combination_strategies.rationale: RHO2 blockade lowers NRF2 activity, increasing ROS susceptibility to APR‑246
combination_strategies.phenotype: Enhanced tumor regression in TNBC PDX (p<0.01)

open_questions: What is the ligand (if any) that drives RHO1/2 signaling in TNBC?
Can anti‑RHO antibodies be humanized for clinical development?
